question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: HederaNetwork.nodes()] Can an unprivileged user set network to HederaNetwork.OTHER, causing nodes() to return an empty Set, leaving the monitor with zero valid nodes and freezing all transaction submission for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: HederaNetwork.nodes()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario where Client.forName() throws an exception during the lazy initialization of HederaNetwork.nodes(), causing @SneakyThrows to propagate a runtime exception that crashes the node discovery phase and freezes all transaction routing for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MirrorNodeProperties.GrpcProperties.getEndpoint()] Can an unprivileged user set mirrorNode.grpc.host to 'in-process:fake', causing getEndpoint() to return an in-process stub address that never connects to a real gRPC server, blocking all transaction status subscriptions and freezing block processing for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MirrorNodeProperties.RestProperties.getBaseUrl()] Can an unprivileged user set mirrorNode.rest.host to an attacker-controlled server that responds with HTTP 200 but returns empty or malformed transaction data, causing the monitor to believe transactions are pending indefinitely and delaying block processing beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged user supply an operator accountId with an extremely large numeric shard value (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE) that causes Long.valueOf() to succeed but the comparison Long.valueOf(accountId[0]) == shard to always fail due to autoboxing equality issues, causing init() to always throw IllegalArgumentException and freezing all transaction processing?

(empty third segment) so that accountId[2] is an empty string, causing operator.setAccountId() to format an account ID like '0.0.' that is invalid for the Hedera SDK, breaking all transaction signing and freezing block processing for 500% or more of average block time?
